sessions.notes: Cheat-asm removal pass. HEAD has 3 cheat-asm constructs (a1val pin, arg0 pin + barrier, asm volatile barrier — see candidate.c header). HEAD byte-matches via these cheats. Stripping the cheats holds sandbox masked-score at 1, but the residual is a 2-instruction branch-sense flip GCC's branch-inversion peephole imposes. Original commit message (9490fd3a) explicitly identifies the barrier as defeating this peephole.

levers_tested.form: drop a1 pin + a0 pin/barrier + asm volatile barrier only
inverted outer if: test (val != dir) first
do { } while(a1val != 0) loop instead of goto loop
move done: label before next: in source order
levers_tested.verdict: no effect — label position doesn't drive BB ordering here

levers_tested.form: explicit goto loop_end + done: return + next: loop-end

remaining_gap: 2 byte diffs at masked insns 48 + 50: target emits `bnez v0, done; j next` (conditional to function-exit, fall-through to loop continuation), mine emits `beqz v0, next; j done`. Semantically equivalent, GCC chose opposite branch sense. The original commit (9490fd3a) named this 'GCC's branch-inversion peephole' and used `asm volatile("")` to defeat it — cheat-asm per current policy.
next_hypotheses: Run permuter from candidate.c base — random C structural mutations may surface a form that flips GCC's BB layout decision
Build instrumented cc1 (BB2_REORG_DEBUG / BB2_JUMP_DEBUG) to dump jump.c's invert_jump decision point; identify exact source-driven trigger
Inspect Kengo (PS2 successor) for any source-form clue on this function — it's renamed from kengo:HIGH nm_cpu/cpu_check_same_dir_timer

Cross-reference matched sibling: `coli_HitPauseKatana_2` (split-read-defeats-hoist) and other code6cac_b.c functions with similar if-goto-done + fall-through-next patterns — find one matched in pure C and copy its emitted form

rejected_forms.reason: do { ... } while (a1val != 0) emits +4 insns (entry-test prologue); regresses to 67 insns vs 63
